content.text:
[Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

CHG-0843 v2: Close TRIGGER-03 and validate OC2A commissioning as live PROD HIVE lead node.

You are Forge (infra). Execute only. Do not plan or ask clarifying questions.

Model available locally is `gemma4:latest` (9.6 GB). Use this tag for the benchmark. Do NOT use `gemma4:26b` unless it is already pulled.

Required outputs:
1. Read `state/chg-triggers.json`. Update the top-level trigger objects only (do not alter sub-action arrays):
   - TRIGGER-01: status → "not_applicable", set `firedAt` null, add/update `closureNote`: "OC2-A/B arrival gate closed; only OC2A commissioned as single-node PROD. HA pair deferred. See CHG-1783."
   - TRIGGER-02: status → "not_applicable", `firedAt` null, `closureNote`: "HA pair not commissioned; OC2A running single-node PROD. HA deferred."
   - TRIGGER-03: status → "fired", `firedAt` → current AEST ISO-8601 timestamp, `validatedBy` → "forge/infra", `closureNote`: "OC2A validated as live PROD HIVE lead node: Mac16,11, 48GB RAM, Tailscale 100.123.95.47. CHG-1783."
   Preserve all other fields and other triggers exactly.
2. Run a local benchmark of `ollama/gemma4:latest` on OC2A. Produce `state/chg0843-gemma4-26b-oc2a-benchmark.json` containing: `timestamp` (AEST ISO-8601), `modelTag`, `prompt`, `tokensPerSecond` (approx), `loadLatencyMs`, `status` (ok/degraded/fail), `errors` (string or null). Use a short prompt like "Say hello in one sentence." and measure generation time. If Ollama is not running, start it first or report status fail with error.
3. Read `MEMORY.md` and `TOOLS.md`. If OC1 is described as live PROD or OC2A as incoming/dev-only, correct so OC2A = LIVE Production HIVE lead and OC1 = Dev/test environment. Do not change other content. If already correct, set `memoryToolsCorrected` to "na" and note it.
4. Write `state/chg0843-execute-report.json` with: `changedFiles` (array of paths changed), `benchmarkPath`, `memoryToolsCorrected` (true/false/na), `blockers` (array of strings).

Validate every file change with `cat` or `jq` before finishing. Do not edit AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, or governance prompts. Return only the final `state/chg0843-execute-report.json` contents in your completion message.

Begin. Execute the assigned task to completion.

This is a multi-step operation that involves reading, modifying JSON files (`state/chg-triggers.json`), running an infrastructure command (benchmarking `ollama/gemma4:latest` on OC2A), and then conditionally modifying other documentation files (`MEMORY.md`, `TOOLS.md`) before writing a final report.

Since I am operating as "Forge (infra)" and the instructions require executing these changes, I must use the appropriate tools. The process needs to be sequential, involving reading current states first to ensure accuracy for validation steps.

I will structure the execution plan:
1.  **Task 1:** Read `state/chg-triggers.json` and construct the required edits for the trigger objects.
2.  **Task 2:** Execute benchmarking on OC2A using available tools. This will likely require a shell command via `exec`.
3.  **Task 3:** Read `MEMORY.md` and `TOOLS.md` to check/correct status descriptions.
4.  **Task 4:** Assemble the final report into `state/chg0843-execute-report.json`.

I will start by reading the necessary initial files. I need to read all three context files before I can proceed with any modification or comparison, as per best practice and necessity for validation.

First, I will attempt to read the contents of `state/chg-triggers.json`, `MEMORY.md`, and `TOOLS.md`.

Then, I will tackle the benchmark execution, which is a core part of the infrastructure task.<channel|>

  "schema": "2.0",

  "lastUpdatedBy": "Yoda",
  "changeNote": "CHG-0859: Added TRIGGER-01 sub-action 01-CLOUD-OUTAGE-PROBE \u2014 Ollama Cloud outage probe + local OC2 fallback, deferred from CHG-0858 outage-detect no-op.",
  "triggers": {
    "TRIGGER-01": {
      "name": "OC2 Arrival \u2014 Master Gate",

      "status": "pending",
      "firedAt": null,
      "description": "OC2 arrival is now the master gate. All OC2-era actions are sequenced as sub-actions under TRIGGER-01, executed in priority order after hardware is online.",
      "subActions": [

          "id": "01-PHYSICAL",
          "name": "Hardware Setup",

          "action": "Unbox OC2-A + OC2-B. Connect power, network, Tailscale. Verify both nodes reachable on tailnet."
        },
        {
          "id": "01-OPENCLAW",
          "name": "OpenClaw Install on OC2-A/B",
          "priority": "critical",
          "action": "Install OpenClaw v2026.5.27+ on OC2-A (primary) and OC2-B (standby). Configure HIVE HA pair. Deploy tech agent fleet (~10 agents: Yoda, Atlas, Thrawn, Forge, Lando, Mon Mothma, Warden, Ahsoka, Luthen; Shield/Lex/Sage stay here). Krennic parked."
        },
        {
          "id": "01-CLAUDE-RESTORE",
          "name": "CLAUDE RESTORE \u2014 Anthropic API Key Re-provisioning",
          "priority": "high",
          "action": "Ken provides new Anthropic API key. Execute SOP: (1) Store in Keychain as 'ainchors-anthropic-api-key', (2) Add auth.profiles.anthropic:default to openclaw.json, (3) Add anthropic model aliases, (4) Update model-policy.json whitelist, (5) Run validate-fallback-chain.sh, (6) Restore agent primary models from claude-restore-config.json.",
          "linkedChgs": [
            "CHG-0349",
            "CHG-0373",
            "CHG-0393"
          ],
          "linkedTkts": [
            "TKT-0165",
            "TKT-0241"
          ],
          "note": "Folded from standalone TRIGGER-14 per Ken directive 2026-05-29. Claude Restore now gates on OC2 arrival \u2014 no point restoring on OC1 when platform is about to migrate."
        },
        {
          "id": "01-PLATFORM-SEPARATION",
          "name": "Platform Separation \u2014 OC1 Business Standalone",
          "priority": "high",
          "action": "Execute Platform Separation per approved option paper. Phase 1: Wipe OC1, fresh OpenClaw install, deploy 6 business agents (Aria lead + Shield/Lex/Sage duplicated + Spark + Forge-ops). Phase 2: OC2 tech platform validated \u22651 week before OC1 wipe. Phase 3: Cutover Angie/KL team to OC1 business node. Phase 4: Stabilisation.",
          "linkedDocs": [
            "docs/options/Platform-Separation-Business-Stream-Option-Paper-v1.0.md",
            "docs/options/Platform-Separation-Business-Stream-Feasibility-Assessment-v1.0.md"
          ],
          "kenDecisions": [
            "C1: Second OpenClaw license \u2705",
            "C2: Separate Ollama Cloud sub \u2705",
            "C3: New Google Workspace for tech \u2705"

            "No SRE on business node",
            "Governance Triad divergence",
            "Double maintenance overhead"
          ],
          "note": "Platform Separation approved 2026-05-29. Phase 0 prep can start now. Full migration gated on OC2 arrival + validation."
        },
        {
          "id": "01-PG-MIGRATION",
          "name": "PostgreSQL Migration OC1 \u2192 OC2",
          "priority": "high",
          "action": "Restore PG from OC1 backup to OC2-A. Configure HA replication to OC2-B. Validate all 32 tables, ticket state, cost history. Slim PG on OC1 (business-only tables or PG-free)."
        },

        },

          "ticket": "TKT-0228"

        {

          "priority": "high",
          "action": "Design and implement a proper Ollama Cloud outage probe (not localhost:11434). On cloud unreachable, trigger automatic fallback to local OC2 models (gemma4:26b / gemma4:31b-coding-mtp-bf16 per TRIGGER-03). Re-enable outage-detect.sh logic with cloud-aware probe. Validate against model-policy.json and run full outage/standby drill.",
          "dependencies": [

            "TRIGGER-03"
          ],

            "CHG-0858",
            "CHG-0859"
          ],

        {

          "name": "OC2A Fresh Install \u2014 Option 4 Strong Separation",
          "priority": "critical",
          "action": "Execute docs/plans/OC2A-Fresh-Install-Plan-v1.0.md. OC2A becomes self-governing full production Nexus; OC1 demoted to non-privileged sandbox with no admin path to OC2A. Phases sequenced as TKT-0757 through TKT-0762. Begin after 01-PHYSICAL and 01-OPENCLAW complete.",

          ],

            "TKT-0760",

          "note": "Replaces prior Platform Separation sub-action (01-PLATFORM-SEPARATION) which assumed OC1 business-only node. New model: OC2A full production, OC1 sandbox only, strong separation."
        }
      ]
    },
